mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in PAYEMS raises household income expectations and supports earnings revisions, causing higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when payroll growth remains above its recent trend.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month PAYEMS growth does not produce positive 20-day return spreads versus lower growth in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and falling household spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days after the labor market deterioration crosses a threshold.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 4.5% or rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum rises while FEDFUNDS is already moving higher, tighter policy expectations raise discount rates faster than earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months and CPIAUCSL 6-month inflation momentum accelerates
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-acceleration and rate-rise trigger has a nonnegative average 20-day excess return versus baseline, or if removing either series does not reduce predictive performance.

mechanism_narrative: Higher short-run RSAFS growth signals stronger consumer demand and near-term revenue growth for listed firms, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity prices incorporate sales momentum.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum fails to predict higher 5-day S&P 500 returns than negative momentum in at least 55% of walk-forward months, or if Rank IC is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals weaker housing investment and future durable-goods demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days after starts fall through a cyclical weakness threshold.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1000 or declines more than 15% over 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if threshold breaches are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered returns are not below non-triggered returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO momentum indicates improving capacity utilization and cyclical output, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through higher expected operating leverage in industrial and goods-producing sectors.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum has zero or negative average 20-day forward return spread versus negative momentum across rolling 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth combined with low FEDFUNDS creates a growth-with-accommodation regime in which earnings expectations rise while discount rates remain contained, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 2.0% and GDPC1 4-quarter growth is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate and positive-GDP-growth state does not outperform other states over 60 trading days, or if either single proxy alone matches the interaction model performance.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation breakout raises expected policy tightening and compresses equity valuation multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when inflation crosses an upside threshold.
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL inflation breakouts above 3.0% are followed by nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if downside hit rate is below 50% in walk-forward testing.
